title: Visibility rules and package groups
description: Visibility controls which packages may depend on a target, and package_group names a reusable set of packages to grant access to.
body: Use when reasoning about why a dependency is allowed or denied at build time. Do not treat visibility as advisory. A target is depend-able only from packages listed in its visibility, defaulting to the package's own scope; a package_group bundles packages so multiple targets can share one allowlist. Inspect the target's visibility attribute and any referenced group. Validation signals include a build error when an unlisted package depends on a restricted target. Risk boundary: widening visibility is easy to do and hard to undo once consumers appear, so grant access deliberately.
